sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system documentation available for this twin-stick shooter

special_mechanics.name: Twin-Stick Shooting
special_mechanics.description: Players use two directional inputs simultaneously - one to move, one to shoot in independent directions. On SNES, this uses the four main buttons (A, B, X, Y) laid out like a D-pad for intuitive dual-control.

special_mechanics.name: Wave-Based Enemy Spawning
special_mechanics.description: Unlike Robotron: 2084, enemies enter the arena in waves via passages on four edges rather than appearing all at once.

tier_notes: Tier 2 - Empirical/Qualitative data only. This is a twin-stick shooter arcade port with no documented movement physics parameters in available sources. Focus is on gameplay mechanics (shooting, wave spawning, prize collection) rather than precise kinematic values. SNES version benefits from button layout enabling natural dual-control.
sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Super Smash T.V. (SNES)
